chunks.title: Accommodation Overview & Pricing
chunks.text:
# Accommodation Overview & Pricing (2026)

Vietnam accommodation spans every price band. Indicative nightly rates for a double room in **off-peak season**:

| Tier | Hanoi / HCMC | Hoi An / Da Nang | Sapa / Ninh Binh | Phu Quoc |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Hostel dorm | 150k–280k VND | 150k–250k VND | 120k–200k VND | 250k–400k VND |
| Budget hotel / 2★ | 400k–700k VND | 400k–650k VND | 350k–550k VND | 500k–900k VND |
| Boutique / 3★ | 800k–1.5M VND | 750k–1.4M VND | 700k–1.2M VND | 1.2M–2.5M VND |
| Luxury / 5★ | 3.5M–8M VND | 3.0M–7M VND | 2.5M–5M VND | 5M–18M VND |
| Resort villa | 6M–20M VND | 5M–18M VND | n/a | 8M–35M VND |

**Peak season** (mid-December to early January, Tet, 30/4–1/5, mid-June to August for domestic tourists): expect +30–80% mark-up; book 60+ days ahead.

**Cancellation tip**: most properties allow free cancellation up to 24–72h before check-in if booked direct on the hotel's website at the same rate as Booking.com or Agoda — and direct booking often includes a free upgrade.

chunks.title: Homestays & Local Experiences
chunks.text:
# Homestays & Local Experiences

**Where homestays shine**:
- **Sapa region** (Ta Van, Lao Chai, Bac Ha): Hmong / Dao villages, USD 15–30 with dinner.
- **Mai Chau** (Hoa Binh): Thai stilt houses, 3h drive from Hanoi, USD 12–25.
- **Pu Luong nature reserve** (Thanh Hoa): rice paddies + mountains, USD 30–60.
- **Mekong Delta** (Ben Tre, Vinh Long, Can Tho): river island homestays, USD 20–40.

**Booking**: small homestays may not be on Booking.com — use VietnamStay, Local Vietnam, or contact via Facebook page. Always confirm:
- Toilet (squat vs Western, ensuite vs shared).
- Hot water availability.
- Wi-Fi (often absent in remote homestays).
- Whether meals are included.

**Etiquette**:
- Remove shoes before entering main house.
- Ask before photographing family altar / sleeping areas.
- Bring small gifts (school supplies, fruit) instead of cash tips.
- Drink the rice wine offered (or pretend convincingly).

chunks.title: Hotel Booking Tips
chunks.text:
# Hotel Booking Tips

**Platforms**:
- **Agoda** — strongest in Asia, frequent flash sales, integrates Vietnamese hotels well.
- **Booking.com** — broadest inventory, free-cancellation default.
- **Direct booking** — best for boutique / luxury; often includes upgrade or breakfast.
- **Klook** — bundles room + tour, e.g. Halong cruise + Hanoi hotel.

**Things to confirm before booking**:
- **Location**: Hanoi Old Quarter < 1 km radius is best for first-time visitors. HCMC District 1 (Ben Thanh / Dong Khoi area) for nightlife; District 3 quieter. Hoi An: inside Ancient Town (no cars) for atmosphere, An Bang Beach for relaxation.
- **Noise**: ground floors next to karaoke bars are common pain points; check recent reviews for "noisy".
- **Tax & breakfast**: rate may exclude 10% VAT + 5% service. Check whether breakfast is included.
- **Wi-Fi speed**: reviews mention this if it's slow.
- **AC quality**: a real concern in summer — confirm split AC, not portable.

**Avoid**: deeply discounted hotels with <10 reviews and no recent ones — common in newly opened low-quality buildings.

chunks.title: Vietnam Top Destinations Overview
chunks.text:
# Vietnam Top Destinations Overview

Vietnam has six widely-recommended destination clusters for first-time international visitors:

chunks.title: Vietnam Top Destinations Overview
chunks.text:
1. **Hanoi (North)** — capital city; Old Quarter, Hoan Kiem Lake, Train Street, Bat Trang ceramic village. Best months: October–April (cool, dry). Avoid July–August (hot, humid, occasional typhoons).
2. **Ha Long Bay & Lan Ha Bay (North)** — 1,600 limestone karst islets; UNESCO World Heritage. Day cruises from Halong city or 2D1N / 3D2N overnight cruises from Tuan Chau / Got pier. Lan Ha is the quieter southern extension accessible from Cat Ba island.
3. **Sapa & Northwest Highlands** — rice terraces, ethnic minority villages (Hmong, Dao). Best months: September–November (clear terraces) and March–May (green planting season). Reach via overnight sleeper train Hanoi → Lao Cai then 1h shuttle.
4. **Hue & Hoi An (Central)** — Hue: Nguyen dynasty imperial citadel + royal tombs. Hoi An: lantern-lit Ancient Town, tailor shops, An Bang beach. Hue ↔ Hoi An via Hai Van Pass (3h scenic drive) or rail.
5. **Da Nang (Central)** — beach city, Marble Mountains, Ba Na Hills (Golden Bridge). Modern infrastructure; convenient base between Hue and Hoi An.
6. **Ho Chi Minh City & Mekong Delta (South)** — HCMC for nightlife, history (War Remnants Museum, Cu Chi Tunnels). Mekong Delta day trips (Ben Tre, My Tho) or overnight stay (Can Tho floating markets).

chunks.title: Vietnam Top Destinations Overview
chunks.text: Other notable destinations: **Phu Quoc** (beach island, 50-minute flight from HCMC, visa-free for 30 days), **Ninh Binh** (Trang An boat ride, often called "Ha Long on land"), **Dalat** (cool highland city, flower farms, coffee), **Phong Nha** (largest cave system in the world, Quang Binh province).

chunks.title: Hanoi — Detailed Guide
chunks.text:
# Hanoi — Detailed Guide

**Recommended duration**: 2–3 days for first-time visitors.

**Must-see**:
- **Hoan Kiem Lake & Ngoc Son Temple** — sunrise walk, free entry to lakeside; temple ticket 30,000 VND.
- **Old Quarter (36 Streets)** — each street historically named after the trade (silk, silver, paper). Stay around Ma May / Hang Bac for walking access.
- **Temple of Literature** — Vietnam's first national university (1070). Ticket 30,000 VND. Allow 1–1.5 hours.
- **Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum complex** — open mornings only (Tue–Thu, Sat, Sun 8:00–11:00); closed Sept–Nov for maintenance. Dress code: covered shoulders + knees.
- **Train Street** (officially closed but cafés still operate) — train passes ~19:30 and 06:00. Sit only inside a registered café.
- **Water Puppet Theatre** — 1-hour show at Thang Long Theatre, tickets 100k–200k VND.

**Food**: Pho Bat Dan (Pho bo), Bun Cha Huong Lien (Obama's stop), Banh Mi 25, Egg Coffee at Giang Café, Bia Hoi corner at Ta Hien.

**Day trips**: Bat Trang ceramic village (45 min, 30k VND bus 47), Duong Lam ancient village (1.5h drive), Perfume Pagoda (2h drive + boat).

chunks.title: Ha Long Bay — Cruise Guide
chunks.text:
# Ha Long Bay — Cruise Guide

**Why cruise (vs day trip)**: overnight cruises reach quieter Bai Tu Long area, include kayaking, swimming, sunset on deck. Day trips stay in the crowded central bay.

**Cruise tiers (2026 prices, per person)**:
- Budget 2D1N (3-star): 2.0–3.5 mUSD VND equivalent ≈ 2,000,000–3,500,000 VND.
- Mid-range 2D1N (4-star, e.g. Paradise Elegance, Halong Sails): 4,500,000–6,500,000 VND.
- Luxury 2D1N (5-star, e.g. Heritage Bình Chuẩn, Stellar of the Seas): 8,000,000–14,000,000 VND.
- 3D2N adds Lan Ha / Cat Ba island stop; +50% over 2D1N price.

**Booking tips**:
- Avoid booking on the pier — quality varies widely; book through Klook, Viator, or Vietnam-based agents (Sinh Tourist, The Sinh Café).
- Confirm what's included: kayak rental, cooking class, transfer from Hanoi (4h coach), entrance fee (290,000 VND/person).
- Off-season (April–June, Sept–Oct) often gives 20–30% discount.

**Weather windows**:
- Best: October–April (cool, dry, calm sea).
- Avoid: July–August (typhoon risk; cruises cancelled with full refund usually).

**Reach from Hanoi**: 2.5–3.5h by coach (Limousine van service ~350k VND one way), or new highway 4h via local bus (~150k VND).

chunks.title: Hoi An — Ancient Town Guide
chunks.text:
# Hoi An — Ancient Town Guide

**Recommended duration**: 2 full days + 1 night to see the lantern festival (15th of each lunar month).

**Highlights**:
- **Ancient Town walking tour** — Japanese Covered Bridge, Tan Ky old house, Fukian Assembly Hall. Combination ticket 120,000 VND covers 5 of 22 attractions, valid 24h.
- **An Bang Beach** — 4 km from town centre, calmer than Cua Dai (which is suffering erosion). Free beach chairs if you order a drink.
- **Tra Que Vegetable Village** — 3 km from town; cooking classes 750k–1.5M VND/person including market tour.
- **My Son Sanctuary** — Cham ruins, 1 hour drive; tour ~600k VND including transport + guide.
- **Lantern release on Thu Bon River** — 20,000 VND for a small lantern; ethically sourced biodegradable lanterns increasingly available.

**Tailor shops**: Yaly Couture, BeBe Tailor, A Dong Silk are well-reviewed. Allow 24–48h for first fitting; plan 3 days minimum if you want a suit or dress.

**Transport**: bicycles are free at most hotels; motorbike rental 150k VND/day. Taxi to Da Nang airport ~400k VND (45 min).

chunks.title: Phu Quoc — Beach Island Guide
chunks.text:
# Phu Quoc — Beach Island Guide

**Why go**: Vietnam's largest island, ~50-minute flight from HCMC or Hanoi, **30-day visa-free for most nationalities** (separate Phu Quoc-specific exemption — see visa.md).

**Best beaches**:
- **Sao Beach** (south) — fine white sand, calm water, busy weekends.
- **Long Beach** (west) — 20 km strip, sunset views, most resorts located here.
- **Ong Lang** (mid-west) — quieter alternative to Long Beach, mid-range resorts.
- **Starfish Beach (Bai Sao Bien)** (northwest) — shallow water, named for visible starfish (touch them gently, do NOT lift out of water).

**Activities**:
- Hon Thom cable car (world's longest over sea, 7.9 km) — 600,000 VND adult, includes Aquatopia waterpark.
- Snorkelling 3-island tour from An Thoi pier — 500k–800k VND.
- VinWonders + Safari combo — 1.6M VND adult, full day.
- Night market in Duong Dong — seafood, pearl jewellery.

**Best months**: November–April (dry). May–October is monsoon — rough seas, many tours cancelled.

**Avoid**: signing up for "free" sightseeing tours that end at a sales pitch for pearl shops or sim resorts.

chunks.title: Sapa — Trekking & Ethnic Villages
chunks.text:
# Sapa — Trekking & Ethnic Villages

**Reach from Hanoi**: overnight sleeper train (Hanoi → Lao Cai, 8h, ~500k VND for soft sleeper) then 1h shuttle to Sapa town (90k VND), OR direct sleeper bus (6h, 400k VND).

**Best trekking villages**:
- **Cat Cat** — closest (3 km), heavily touristic, paved path.
- **Lao Chai → Ta Van** — classic full-day trek (12 km, 6h), passes through Hmong and Giay villages.
- **Y Linh Ho → Lao Chai → Ta Van** — harder 1-day trek.
- **Ban Ho → Nam Toong → Thanh Phu** — 2-day homestay trek, less touristy.

**Best months**:
- **September–November**: golden rice terraces, clear weather.
- **March–May**: green planting season, cooler.
- **December–February**: cold (5–10°C), mist, occasional snow on Fansipan.

**Fansipan summit**:
- Cable car: 800,000 VND round trip + 70,000 VND funicular to base. Allow 4 hours.
- Trekking: 2D1N moderate difficulty; needs licensed guide (~3,500,000 VND including porter).

**Homestay etiquette**:
- Remove shoes before entering.
- Bring small gifts (notebooks, pens) for host's children rather than candy.
- Tip 50k–100k VND per guest per night beyond agreed rate.

chunks.title: Vietnamese Food — Must-Try Staples
chunks.text:
# Vietnamese Food — Must-Try Staples

**Pho** — rice noodle soup with beef (Pho bo) or chicken (Pho ga). Best regional differences: Hanoi pho is clear-broth with simple garnish (green onion, cilantro). Saigon pho is sweeter, served with a plate of bean sprouts, lime, sliced chilli, basil.

**Bun cha** — Hanoi specialty: grilled pork patties + pork belly in sweet-savoury fish-sauce broth, dipped with rice vermicelli and herbs. Bun Cha Huong Lien (Obama's stop) is famous; Bun Cha Dac Kim (Hang Manh St) is locals' favourite.

**Banh mi** — French baguette + Vietnamese fillings (pâté, cold cuts, pickled carrot/daikon, cilantro, chilli). Top picks: Banh Mi Phuong (Hoi An), Banh Mi 25 (Hanoi), Banh Mi Huynh Hoa (HCMC, line is long but real).

**Bun bo Hue** — spicy beef noodle soup, Central Vietnam. Annam Bun Bo Hue and Quan O Xuan (Hue) are classics.

**Cao lau** — Hoi An only; chewy noodles + pork + greens + crackers. Authenticity tied to local well water; outside Hoi An it's just imitation.

**Banh xeo** — crispy turmeric rice-flour pancake with shrimp + pork + bean sprouts. Eat wrapped in lettuce + herbs, dipped in fish sauce.

chunks.title: Vietnamese Food — Must-Try Staples
chunks.text:
**Goi cuon** — fresh spring rolls with shrimp/pork, herbs, rice vermicelli in rice paper. Dip in peanut hoisin sauce.

**Com tam** — broken rice + grilled pork chop + egg + pickled vegetables. Southern Vietnam comfort food. Com Tam Ba Ghien (HCMC) is iconic.

**Egg coffee (ca phe trung)** — Hanoi invention, egg yolk whipped with condensed milk on top of strong Vietnamese coffee. Try at Cafe Giang.

chunks.title: Street Food Safety
chunks.text:
# Street Food Safety

**Rules of thumb**:
- Choose stalls with high turnover and visible queue of locals — food doesn't sit out.
- Watch the cook prepare your dish; freshly cooked > pre-prepared.
- Avoid raw vegetables / ice if you have a sensitive stomach the first few days. Most reputable urban shops use bottled ice (look for cylindrical ice with a hole in the middle).
- Skip raw blood pudding (tiết canh) and undercooked pork — small but real food poisoning / parasite risk.
- Drink bottled or filtered water. Tap water in Vietnam is NOT safe to drink.

**Bring**: hand sanitiser, antidiarrheal (loperamide) for emergencies, rehydration sachets (oresol). Pharmacies are everywhere and cheap.

**Allergy phrases**:
- Tôi bị dị ứng với đậu phộng. = I'm allergic to peanuts.
- Tôi bị dị ứng với hải sản. = I'm allergic to seafood.
- Tôi ăn chay. = I'm vegetarian. (Note: even "vegetarian" dishes may contain fish sauce — say "ăn chay trường" for strict vegan.)
- Không cay. = Not spicy.

chunks.title: Regional Cuisine Differences
chunks.text:
# Regional Cuisine Differences

**Northern (Hanoi, Halong, Sapa)** — restrained, less sweet, less spicy. Heavier use of black pepper, ginger, garlic. Soups dominate: pho, bun cha, bun thang. Influence from China.

**Central (Hue, Hoi An, Da Nang)** — most complex. Hue was the imperial capital — many tiny composed dishes (banh beo, banh bot loc, banh nam). Spicier than the north (chilli, lemongrass).

**Southern (HCMC, Mekong Delta)** — sweeter (palm sugar), more coconut milk, more fresh herbs, more tropical fruit. Banh xeo, hu tieu, ca kho to (caramelised fish in clay pot).

**Highland (Da Lat, Sapa, Buon Ma Thuot)** — barbecued meats, fresh vegetables, fermented foods. Specialty: thang co (Mong stew), com lam (rice in bamboo), Da Lat artichoke tea.

chunks.title: 7-Day Classic Vietnam Itinerary
chunks.text:
# 7-Day Classic Vietnam Itinerary

Best for first-time visitors who want the highlights of north + central.

**Day 1 — Arrive Hanoi**: airport transfer to Old Quarter hotel. Evening walk to Hoan Kiem Lake, dinner Bun Cha or Pho.

**Day 2 — Hanoi city**: morning Temple of Literature + Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum complex (closes 11:00). Afternoon Old Quarter walking + egg coffee at Cafe Giang. Evening Water Puppet show (Thang Long Theatre).

**Day 3 — Halong Bay 2D1N cruise**: pickup 08:00 from hotel, ~3h drive. Lunch onboard, kayaking, sunset on deck.

**Day 4 — Halong cruise day 2**: morning cave + Ti Top island swim, lunch onboard, return Hanoi by 17:00. Evening overnight train to Hue (option) or fly to Da Nang next morning.

**Day 5 — Fly to Da Nang / drive to Hoi An**: 45min flight Hanoi → DAD, 45min taxi DAD → Hoi An (400k VND). Afternoon Ancient Town walking + lantern release on Thu Bon river.

**Day 6 — Hoi An full day**: morning Tra Que vegetable village + cooking class. Afternoon free for tailor fitting / beach. Evening night market.

**Day 7 — Hoi An → My Son → depart**: morning My Son Cham ruins half-day tour. Afternoon DAD airport transfer + flight home.

chunks.title: 7-Day Classic Vietnam Itinerary
chunks.text:
**Indicative budget** (per person, 2026, mid-range double occupancy):
- Hotels (6 nights): 4.5M VND.
- Halong cruise 2D1N (4★): 5M VND.
- Domestic flight HAN → DAD: 1M VND.
- Tours + entrance: 2.5M VND.
- Food + transfer: 2.5M VND.
- **Total per person: ~15.5M VND (≈ USD 620)**, excluding international flight.

chunks.title: 10-Day North + South Itinerary
chunks.text:
# 10-Day North + South Itinerary

Adds Sapa + HCMC + Mekong to the classic route.

**Day 1**: Arrive Hanoi.
**Day 2**: Hanoi city.
**Day 3**: Overnight train to Sapa (departs ~22:00).
**Day 4**: Arrive Sapa, half-day Lao Chai → Ta Van trek. Homestay night.
**Day 5**: Cat Cat or Fansipan cable car. Evening sleeper bus or train back to Hanoi (or fly next morning from Lao Cai's nearest airport at Dien Bien).
**Day 6**: Arrive Hanoi, fly to Da Nang (afternoon), transfer Hoi An.
**Day 7**: Hoi An full day.
**Day 8**: Fly HAN → SGN (1.5h). Afternoon Cu Chi Tunnels tour. Evening rooftop drink at Bitexco / Landmark 81.
**Day 9**: Mekong Delta day trip (Ben Tre or My Tho).
**Day 10**: HCMC War Remnants Museum + Reunification Palace, depart.

**Indicative budget**: ~22M VND per person (≈ USD 880) excluding international flight.

chunks.title: 14-Day Grand Vietnam Itinerary
chunks.text:
# 14-Day Grand Vietnam Itinerary

For travellers wanting depth + beach time. Pace: 1 region every 4–5 days.

**Days 1–4 — North**: Hanoi (2 days) + Halong cruise (2 days, 1 night). Optional: add Sapa 2 days (-2 from southern leg).
**Days 5–8 — Central**: train or fly Hanoi → Hue. Hue 2 days (imperial citadel, royal tombs). Drive Hai Van Pass → Da Nang/Hoi An, 2 days (Ancient Town, beach, cooking class).
**Days 9–11 — South**: fly Da Nang → HCMC. HCMC 1.5 days + Mekong Delta 1.5 days (overnight Ben Tre or Can Tho).
**Days 12–14 — Beach finale**: fly HCMC → Phu Quoc. 3 days beach + Hon Thom cable car + sunset.

**Indicative budget**: ~32M VND per person (≈ USD 1,280) for mid-range, ~55M VND (USD 2,200) for 4★+ private guide.

chunks.title: General Safety in Vietnam
chunks.text:
# General Safety in Vietnam

Vietnam is generally safe for foreign visitors. Violent crime against tourists is rare. The main risks are **opportunistic petty theft**, **traffic**, and **food/water-borne illness**.

**Petty theft hotspots**:
- HCMC District 1 (Bui Vien, Dong Khoi) — drive-by phone snatching from motorbike. Hold your phone with two hands and away from the road edge.
- Hanoi Old Quarter — pickpocketing during the weekend walking street.
- Beach resorts (Mui Ne, Nha Trang) — leave nothing on the beach when swimming.

**Traffic**: Vietnam has one of the highest road-fatality rates in Southeast Asia. As a pedestrian: walk slowly and steadily across the road; let motorbikes weave around you. Do NOT stop or run.

**Scams to know**:
- "Free" cyclo / xich lo ride that demands USD 50 at the end. Agree price in writing first.
- "Tour" / "tailor" touts at airport claiming your hotel is closed. Walk past.
- Taxi meter running 3x normal speed in airport pickups — use Grab or the airport bus.
- Fake monks blessing bracelets and demanding payment. Politely decline.

chunks.title: Health & Vaccinations
chunks.text:
# Health & Vaccinations for Vietnam

**Routine vaccinations**: ensure MMR, Tdap, polio, influenza are up to date.

**Recommended for most travellers**:
- **Hepatitis A** — food/water-borne, recommended for all visitors.
- **Typhoid** — especially if eating street food extensively.
- **Tetanus booster** if last dose >10 years ago.

**Recommended for longer stays / rural travel**:
- **Hepatitis B** — long-term travellers, medical exposure risk.
- **Japanese Encephalitis** — long rural stays in rice-growing areas during monsoon.
- **Rabies pre-exposure** — long stays, frequent contact with animals.

**Malaria**: low risk in most tourist areas (HCMC, Hanoi, Halong, Hoi An, Da Nang are malaria-free). Some risk in rural Central Highlands and Mekong Delta provinces. Consult CDC / WHO maps for current guidance.

**Dengue**: present year-round, peak May–November. Use DEET-based repellent (30%+). No vaccine for tourists.

**Travel insurance**: strongly recommended. Confirm coverage includes:
- Hospital evacuation (USD 200,000+).
- Motorbike accidents (often excluded unless you hold a valid IDP licence).
- COVID-related cancellations.

chunks.title: Health & Vaccinations
chunks.text:
**Hospitals**:
- Hanoi: Vinmec Times City, Hanoi French Hospital.
- HCMC: FV Hospital (Phu My Hung), Vinmec Central Park.
- Da Nang: Family Medical Practice.

chunks.title: Emergency Numbers & Embassies
chunks.text:
# Emergency Numbers & Embassies

**National emergency**:
- Police: 113
- Fire: 114
- Ambulance: 115

**Tourist police hotlines** (English speakers):
- Hanoi: 0913-454-068
- HCMC: 0903-739-188
- Da Nang: 0511-3550-111

**Major embassies in Hanoi**:
- USA: 7 Lang Ha St, Ba Dinh; +84-24-3850-5000.
- UK: Central Building, 31 Hai Ba Trung; +84-24-3936-0500.
- Australia: 8 Dao Tan St; +84-24-3774-0100.

**Major consulates in HCMC**:
- USA: 4 Le Duan; +84-28-3520-4200.
- UK: 25 Le Duan; +84-28-3825-1380.

Always carry: passport photocopy, embassy phone number, insurance card, ~500k VND emergency cash separate from main wallet.

chunks.title: Domestic Flights in Vietnam
chunks.text:
# Domestic Flights in Vietnam

**Main carriers**: Vietnam Airlines (full-service), Vietjet Air (budget), Bamboo Airways (mid-tier). Pacific Airlines (Vietnam Airlines' budget subsidiary) operates a smaller schedule.

**Booking tips**:
- Book 30–60 days ahead for the cheapest fares; 7 days ahead can double the price.
- Vietjet sells "Eco" fares without baggage — add 20 kg checked bag online for ~250k VND, vs ~600k VND at the airport counter.
- Tet (Lunar New Year, late Jan – mid Feb) and 30/4–1/5 (Reunification Day) holidays sell out 60+ days ahead.

**Common routes & 2026 indicative one-way fares (economy, 30 days advance)**:
- Hanoi (HAN) ↔ HCMC (SGN): 1.5M–2.5M VND.
- Hanoi (HAN) ↔ Da Nang (DAD): 700k–1.5M VND.
- HCMC (SGN) ↔ Da Nang (DAD): 800k–1.6M VND.
- HCMC (SGN) ↔ Phu Quoc (PQC): 600k–1.3M VND.
- Hanoi (HAN) ↔ Phu Quoc (PQC): 1.5M–2.8M VND.

**Airport transfer**:
- Noi Bai (HAN): airport bus 86 (45k VND, 45 min to Old Quarter), Grab car (350k–450k VND).
- Tan Son Nhat (SGN): airport bus 49 (40k VND, 40 min to District 1), Grab car (200k–280k VND). Avoid pre-arranged taxi touts inside the arrivals hall.
- Da Nang (DAD): 10-minute Grab to city centre, ~80k VND.

chunks.title: Vietnam Train Travel
chunks.text:
# Vietnam Train Travel

The **Reunification Express (Hanoi → HCMC, 1,726 km)** runs four pairs of trains daily (SE1/SE2 … SE7/SE8). Total journey 30–34 hours; most travellers do segments rather than end-to-end.

**Popular segments (2026 indicative prices, soft sleeper 4-berth, one way)**:
- Hanoi → Lao Cai (for Sapa): 8h overnight, 500k–700k VND. Tourist-class carriages: Chapa Express, Victoria Express, Orient Express (+300k–500k vs state-run).
- Hanoi → Hue: 13h overnight, 700k–900k VND.
- Hanoi → Da Nang: 17h, 800k–1.0M VND.
- Hue → Da Nang: 3h, 100k–150k VND (sit in coach for the Hai Van Pass coastal views).
- Da Nang → Nha Trang: 9h, 400k–700k VND.
- Nha Trang → HCMC: 8h, 350k–600k VND.

**Booking**: dsvn.vn (Vietnam Railways official, accepts foreign cards), or Baolau / 12go.asia for English UI (~10% mark-up).

**Tip**: book 30+ days ahead for Sapa-bound overnight trains, especially weekends.

chunks.title: Long-Distance Coach & Limousine
chunks.text:
# Long-Distance Coach & Limousine

**Coach types**:
- **Sleeper coach (Xe giường nằm)** — 3 rows of bunks, used for overnight 6–12h routes. Popular operators: Phuong Trang (Futa Bus), Hoang Long, Sinh Tourist.
- **Limousine van (9-seat)** — premium 9-seat vans, doorstep pick-up, used for 2–4h routes (Hanoi ↔ Ninh Binh / Halong / Sapa). Price ~2x coach.
- **Open Tour Bus** — Hanoi ↔ Hue ↔ Hoi An ↔ Nha Trang ↔ HCMC tickets, hop-on hop-off style. Sinh Tourist most established.

**2026 indicative prices, one way**:
- Hanoi → Sapa (sleeper): 350k–450k VND, 6h.
- Hanoi → Ha Long (limousine): 280k–350k VND, 2.5h.
- Hanoi → Ninh Binh (limousine): 200k–280k VND, 2h.
- HCMC → Mui Ne (sleeper): 200k–300k VND, 5h.
- HCMC → Da Lat (sleeper or limousine): 280k–400k VND, 7h.
- Hue → Hoi An (open tour): 150k–200k VND, 3h via Hai Van Pass.

**Safety**: choose well-known operators only; sleeper coach accidents do happen on mountain roads at night.

chunks.title: Getting Around in Cities
chunks.text:
# Getting Around in Cities

**Ride-hailing**: **Grab** is dominant nationwide; **Be** and **Xanh SM** (electric, owned by Vingroup) are credible alternatives. All accept cash or in-app card. Avoid hailing street taxis other than Mai Linh or Vinasun (HCMC) — meter-tampering common.

**Motorbike taxi (xe ôm)**: Grab Bike or Be Bike are cheap and bypass traffic; helmet provided. Sit upright, hold the side handle (not the driver).

**Renting a scooter**: ~120k–180k VND/day for 110cc automatic. Note:
- Foreign drivers technically need a Vietnamese-recognised licence (IDP 1968 Convention; Vietnam ratified in 2014). Police checks are common in Hoi An, Da Nang, Mui Ne — fine 800k–1.2M VND for unlicensed.
- Travel insurance often voids coverage for scooter accidents without a valid licence.

**Metro**:
- Hanoi: line 2A (Cat Linh ↔ Ha Dong) and line 3 (Nhon ↔ Hanoi Station) operating, 8k–15k VND.
- HCMC: line 1 (Ben Thanh ↔ Suoi Tien) opened December 2024, 7k–20k VND.

**Bicycle**: most hotels in Hoi An, Hue, Ninh Binh provide free bikes. HCMC and Hanoi NOT recommended for inexperienced cyclists due to traffic.

chunks.title: Vietnam Visa Overview (2026)
chunks.text:
# Vietnam Visa Overview (2026)

Vietnam offers three main entry channels for foreigners:

1. **Visa exemption** — 15-day to 90-day visa-free entry for citizens of selected countries.
2. **E-visa** — single or multiple-entry online visa, up to 90 days, available to all 80+ nationalities since August 2023.
3. **Visa-on-arrival (VOA)** — requires a pre-approval letter from a sponsor agency; mostly for air travellers landing at Hanoi, HCMC, Da Nang, Cam Ranh, Phu Quoc.

**Recommendation**: most leisure travellers should use the **e-visa** (cheapest, no agency middleman). VOA is mostly relevant for visa changes mid-trip or for travellers from agencies' partner countries.

chunks.title: Visa Exemption List
chunks.text:
# Visa Exemption List (as of 2026)

**45 days, single entry** (extended from 15 to 45 days in August 2023):
- Most Western European countries: UK, France, Germany, Italy, Spain, Sweden, Denmark, Norway, Finland, Belarus, Russia.
- Japan, South Korea.

**21 days, single entry**:
- Philippines.

**14 days, single entry**:
- Brunei, Myanmar.

**30 days, single entry**:
- All ASEAN nationalities (Indonesia, Malaysia, Singapore, Thailand, Laos, Cambodia).

**Phu Quoc-only 30-day exemption** — applies to **all** nationalities visiting Phu Quoc island only:
- Must enter and exit directly via Phu Quoc (international flight or boat).
- Cannot continue to other parts of Vietnam without obtaining a separate visa.
- Children listed on parent's passport included.

**For other nationalities** (USA, Australia, Canada, India, China, etc.): no visa exemption — use e-visa.

chunks.title: E-Visa Application Walkthrough
chunks.text:
# E-Visa Application Walkthrough (2026)

**Official portal**: https://evisa.xuatnhapcanh.gov.vn (English available). **Beware** of third-party sites charging USD 50–100 mark-ups.

**Fees** (paid online by international card):
- Single entry: USD 25.
- Multiple entry: USD 50.

**Validity**: up to 90 days, single or multiple entry. Apply 7–14 days before travel; processing time officially 3 working days, in practice 3–5 working days.

**Required**:
- Passport with 6+ months validity remaining from intended date of exit.
- Passport bio page photo (PDF/JPG, <2 MB).
- Recent portrait photo (4×6 cm, white background, <2 MB).
- Intended port of entry and exit (can be changed at border on arrival).
- Address of first accommodation in Vietnam (any registered hotel address is fine).

**Tips**:
- Print 2 copies of the e-visa approval — one for airline check-in, one for immigration.
- Children need their own e-visa (not on parent's).
- E-visa cannot be extended in-country — exit and reapply, or convert to a longer visa via an agency.

chunks.title: Visa Extension Inside Vietnam
chunks.text:
# Visa Extension Inside Vietnam

**Visa-exempt entries** (45-day Western Europeans, 30-day ASEAN, etc.) **cannot be extended** in-country. Options:

1. **Border run** to Cambodia or Laos: cross at a land border, get a new 45-day stamp on return. Common at Moc Bai (HCMC → Phnom Penh, 8h) or Lao Bao (Hue → Savannakhet).
2. **Convert to e-visa**: apply for an e-visa while inside Vietnam, then leave and re-enter to activate. Adds 1 international hop.
3. **Convert to longer visa via agency**: 3-month tourist visa via local agency costs USD 80–150, processed by immigration office in 3–7 days.

**E-visa holders** can extend once for the same duration via licensed agency (USD 70–120 for 30-day extension).

**Overstay penalties**:
- 1–15 days: USD 25 fine at exit.
- 16–30 days: USD 25 fine + interrogation + warning stamp.
- 30+ days: deportation, 1–5 year re-entry ban.
